subject: NSEC
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
questions.q:
$P, Q, R$ and $S$ are four metals whose typical reactions are given below.
(I) Only Q and R react with dilute HCl to give $\mathrm{H}_2$ gas.
(II) When Q is added to a solution containing the ions of the other metals, metallic $\mathrm{P}, \mathrm{R}$ and S are formed.
(III) P reacts with concentrated $\mathrm{HNO}_3$ but S does not.
The correct order of their reducing character is:

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
questions.q:
The following Ellingham diagram depicts the oxidation of ' C ', ' CO ' and ' Fe '. Which of the following is correct?
I. FeO can be reduced by C below 600 K II. FeO can be reduced by CO below 600 K III. FeO can be reduced by C above 1000 K IV. FeO can be reduced by CO above 1000 K

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
questions.q:
Ellingham diagrams are plots of $\Delta \mathrm{G}^0$ vs temperature which have applications in metallurgy. $2 \mathrm{H}_2+\mathrm{O}_2=2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \quad \Delta \mathrm{G}(\mathrm{~J})=-247500+55.85 \mathrm{~T} \ldots \ldots (I)$ $2 \mathrm{CO}+\mathrm{O}_2=2 \mathrm{CO}_2 \quad \Delta \mathrm{G}(\mathrm{~J})=-282400+86.81 \mathrm{~T} \ldots \ldots (II)$ The Ellingham diagrams for the oxidation of $\mathrm{H}_2$ (I) and CO (II) are given below. The two lines intersect $\left(\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{E}}\right)$ at 1125 K .
Which of the following is correct? I. $\Delta \mathrm{G}^{\circ}$ for reaction (i) is more negative at $\mathrm{T} < 1125 \mathrm{~K}$ II. $\Delta \mathrm{G}^{\circ}$ for the reduction of CO is more negative at $\mathrm{T} < 1125 \mathrm{~K}$ III. $\mathrm{H}_2$ is a better reducing agent at $\mathrm{T}>1125 \mathrm{~K}$ IV. $\mathrm{H}_2$ is a better reducing agent at $\mathrm{T} < 1125 \mathrm{~K}$

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
questions.q:
Aluminium and copper are extracted from their oxide and sulphide ores respectively Which of the following is correct?
I. Copper is extracted by the auto reduction of copper oxide by copper sulphide
II. Aluminium cannot be obtained by chemical reduction due to its strong affinity for oxygen
III. In electrometallurgy of Al , graphite is used as cathode to avoid reoxidation of Al into $\mathrm{Al}_2 \mathrm{O}_3$ by preventing formation of $\mathrm{O}_2$.
IV. Sulphide ores of copper are difficult to be reduced than the oxide ores

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
questions.q:
Consider the following statements:
(i) Calcination is carried out in absence of air below the melting point of the ore
(ii) Roasting and calcination are carried out in presence of flux
(iii) Calcination is carried out in limited supply of air above the melting point of the ore
(iv) Roasting is carried out in air below the melting point of ore

The correct set of statements is
